full.summary: Complete organization with governance, projects, knowledge, agents, loops, and maintenance.
full.use_when: Several projects, agents, corpora, or authority boundaries must remain coherent.
full.avoid_when: A single bounded concern can be served by a standalone layer or smaller profile.

organization.summary: Governance and actor model without requiring projects, knowledge, or runtime agents.
organization.use_when: A team or human-agent collaboration needs purpose, authority, privacy, and amendment rules.
organization.avoid_when: Only one project or corpus needs structure.

constitution.summary: Standalone invariants, authority root, prohibited actions, and amendment rules.
constitution.use_when: Only the non-negotiable boundary of a system needs to be made explicit.
constitution.avoid_when: Operational decisions, actors, projects, or knowledge also need durable homes.

identity.summary: Standalone actors, roles, authority, delegation, incentives, and privacy.
identity.use_when: The immediate ambiguity is who may decide, access, delegate, approve, or stop.
identity.avoid_when: No material actor or permission boundary exists.

project.summary: One standalone project with sources, decisions, risks, unknowns, and handoff.
project.use_when: A bounded initiative needs continuity but no broader organization is required.
project.avoid_when: The work is one-off and does not need durable context.

rail.summary: One canonical work ledger with dependencies, claims, review, evidence, and human gates.
rail.use_when: Work must survive a session or cross a human, role, agent, dependency, review, or approval boundary.
rail.avoid_when: A bounded task can be completed and verified in one session without durable coordination.

project-system.summary: A standalone project with reusable procedures, knowledge, and upkeep.
project-system.use_when: A durable project needs its own corpus, procedures, and maintenance without an organization layer.
project-system.avoid_when: The project is short-lived or its knowledge already has a canonical external home.

knowledge.use_when: Claims, sources, contradictions, and unknowns need a durable home but change is human-driven.
knowledge.avoid_when: Correctness decays on an external clock and requires recurring review.

knowledge-system.summary: Curated knowledge plus reusable procedures and maintenance of the system itself.
knowledge-system.use_when: A corpus needs repeatable ingestion, query, audit, and retirement procedures.
knowledge-system.avoid_when: Sources change frequently enough to require governed recurring maintenance.

living-knowledge-system.summary: Knowledge whose correctness decays as external sources change.
living-knowledge-system.use_when: Regulations, markets, provider catalogs, security advisories, or other sources require scheduled review and publication gates.
living-knowledge-system.avoid_when: The corpus changes only when a human deliberately edits it.

llm-wiki.summary: Source-first compiled wiki with inventory, quiet archives, datasets, outputs, and recurring audits.
llm-wiki.use_when: Research sources should compound into indexed articles, queries, and derived outputs across sessions.
llm-wiki.avoid_when: A small set of stable notes does not need compilation or recurring audit.

second-brain.summary: Personal or solo human-agent knowledge system organized around active outcomes.
second-brain.use_when: A person needs capture, organization, distillation, expression, projects, and durable context.
second-brain.avoid_when: The knowledge belongs to a governed team or regulated operational corpus.

agent-team.summary: Multiple installed agents with skills, knowledge, model policy, authority, and upkeep.
agent-team.use_when: Software actors need distinct roles, procedures, knowledge attachments, models, and review gates.
agent-team.avoid_when: One general agent and repository instructions are sufficient.

loop-system.summary: Recurring or long-running agent work with state, budgets, verification, evidence, and human gates.
loop-system.use_when: Work must continue across runs, agents, schedules, or handoffs without relying on chat memory.
loop-system.avoid_when: The task can be completed safely in one bounded session.

graph-system.summary: Canonical Markdown knowledge with rebuildable graph projections and graph-quality checks.
graph-system.use_when: Relationships, dependencies, communities, or navigation matter more than a flat file list.
graph-system.avoid_when: A few documents can be read directly without graph analysis.

need_rules.reason: The work does not appear to need durable organizational structure.
The need concerns durable invariants and amendment boundaries.
The need spans purpose, authority, actors, and system upkeep.
The material ambiguity is who participates and what each actor may do.
The work is a bounded operating unit that needs sources, decisions, risks, and continuity.
Durable work needs one canonical ledger with dependencies, claims, review, evidence, and human gates.
Claims, sources, uncertainty, and retrieval need a durable corpus.
Correctness decays as external sources change, so recurring review and publication gates are justified.
The need is a source-first wiki that compounds across research, query, audit, and output cycles.
The need is a personal, outcome-oriented knowledge system rather than an institutional corpus.
A recurring procedure needs explicit inputs, verification, failure handling, provenance, and risk review.
Model or retrieval selection needs dated capability evidence, constraints, evaluation, and fallback policy.
The need concerns inspection, drift, failure learning, revision, or retirement of the system itself.
Distinct agents need roles, skills, knowledge attachments, model policy, and review gates.
Long-running work needs durable state, budgets, verification, evidence, and human-controlled stop conditions.
Relationships should be projected from canonical documents into a rebuildable graph.
